transcript.final_response:
The result is now correct. Here's the final `/app/.env`:

```
DB_HOST=db.prod.internal
DB_PORT=5432
DB_NAME=myapp
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
LOG_LEVEL=warn
```

**Summary of how each value was resolved:**

| Key | Source | Value |
|------|--------|-------|
| `DB_HOST` | override | `db.prod.internal` |
| `DB_PORT` | template default | `5432` |
| `DB_NAME` | template default | `myapp` |
| `REDIS_URL` | template default | `redis://localhost:6379` |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | override | `warn` |

**Note on the bugs I hit along the way** (worth knowing if you reuse this):
- `${var%\}}` does **not** strip a trailing `}` — the `\}` ends the expansion early and actually *appends* a literal `}`. The reliable fix is to put the brace in a variable (`brace='}'; ${var%$brace}`).
- I also ran into stale `declare -A` state when re-running in the same shell, so I moved the logic into a standalone script run with `bash` to keep each run clean.

The values like `redis://localhost:6379` (containing `:`) were preserved correctly because I split the default only on the *first* `:-` and only stripped a single closing brace.
tool_calls: echo "=== template.env ==="; cat /config/template.env; echo "=== overrides.txt ==="; cat /config/overrides.txt

mkdir -p /app
declare -A overrides
while IFS='=' read -r k v; do
  [[ -z "$k" ]] && continue
  overrides["$k"]="$v"
done < /config/overrides.txt

> /app/.env
while IFS= read -r line; do
  [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
  key="${line%%=*}"
  # extract default: strip ${KEY:- ... } -> portion after :- and before closing }
  rhs="${line#*=}"
  default="${rhs#*:-}"
  default="${default%\}}"
  if [[ -n "${overrides[$key]+x}" ]]; then
    val="${overrides[$key]}"
  else
    val="$default"
  fi
  echo "${key}=${val}" >> /app/.env
done < /config/template.env

echo "=== /app/.env ==="
cat /app/.env

declare -A overrides
while IFS='=' read -r k v; do
  [[ -z "$k" ]] && continue
  overrides["$k"]="$v"
done < /config/overrides.txt

> /app/.env
while IFS= read -r line; do
  [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
  key="${line%%=*}"
  rhs="${line#*=}"          # ${KEY:-default}
  default="${rhs#*:-}"      # default}
  default="${default%\}}"   # strip exactly one trailing }
  if [[ -n "${overrides[$key]+x}" ]]; then
    val="${overrides[$key]}"
  else
    val="$default"
  fi
  echo "${key}=${val}" >> /app/.env
done < /config/template.env

echo "=== /app/.env ==="
cat /app/.env

line='DB_PORT=${DB_PORT:-5432}'
rhs="${line#*=}"
echo "rhs=[$rhs]"
default="${rhs#*:-}"
echo "after :- =[$default]"
default="${default%\}}"
echo "after strip =[$default]"

cat > /tmp/build_env.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p /app

declare -A overrides
while IFS='=' read -r k v; do
  [[ -z "$k" ]] && continue
  overrides["$k"]="$v"
done < /config/overrides.txt

brace='}'
: > /app/.env
while IFS= read -r line; do
  [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
  key="${line%%=*}"
  rhs="${line#*=}"            # ${KEY:-default}
  default="${rhs#*:-}"        # default}
  default="${default%$brace}" # strip exactly one trailing }
  if [[ -n "${overrides[$key]+x}" ]]; then
    val="${overrides[$key]}"
  else
    val="$default"
  fi
  printf '%s=%s\n' "$key" "$val" >> /app/.env
done < /config/template.env
EOF
bash /tmp/build_env.sh
echo "=== /app/.env ==="
cat /app/.env
